name: New Student Initiatives
summary: We help new students at Nevada succeed academically, socially and emotionally in their first year.

name: Student Persistence Research Office
summary: Retention research and assessment office for the Division of Student Services.

name: The Multicultural Center
summary: A critical hub of cultural, LGBTQIA, gender-focused services, programs, and social justice initiatives that support student success and graduation.

name: ASUN IDEA
summary: The ASUN Department of IDEA aims to serve underrepresented student populations on campus.

name: ASUN Programming
summary: The ASUN Department of Programming plans and implements all ASUN events.

name: Dean of Students Office
summary: The Dean of Students Office delivers programs and support to foster a culture of empowerment, care, and accountability for all students.

name: Center for Student Engagement
summary: The Center for Student Engagement works collaboratively to engage, educate and empower undergraduate students to take action in the best interest of the student body and the University community.

name: University Studies Abroad Consortium
summary: USAC offers the opportunity for students to live and learn in countries around the world through study abroad opportunities in over 50+ locations.

name: Undergraduate Research
summary: Undergraduate Research provides funded research opportunities for undergraduates conducting research, scholarship, and creative activity. Free workshops, trainings, and symposia available.

name: Welcome Center, Office for Prospective Students
summary: Home to the University campus visit operation, we offer tours and visit programs to prospective students and their families.

name: Office of International Students and Scholars
summary: Our staff members have extensive knowledge and experience in the international education field and work with students, scholars, and community members to help enrich their study and research experiences at the University.

name: Disability Resource Center
summary: The DRC assists students in negotiating disability related barriers and strives to improve access and opportunity. This enables all levels of students with disabilities to become integrated into campus life, maximize independence and be more successful.

name: Women of Color Collective
summary: A safe, supportive, and welcoming space for Asian & Pacific Islander, Black, Latinx, Middle Eastern/North African, Native American, and other women-identifying people of color.

name: Nevada Career Studio
summary: The Nevada Career Studio’s mission is to connect students, educators, and employers to meet the workforce development needs of the state, nation and global community by building student career readiness from early engagement, graduation and beyond

name: DeLaMare Science and Engineering Library
summary: DLM Design, Learn, Make!

name: American Marketing Association
summary: AMA at the University of Nevada, Reno offers a platform for professional development for students who are interested in marketing, journalism or general business.

name: First Generation Student Center
summary: Helping students overcome barriers to higher education.

name: Brushfire Literature & Arts Journal
summary: The Brushfire Literature & Arts Journal is a student-run organization at the University of Nevada, Reno. Brushfire publishes biannually. All the art that is reviewed and published by Brushfire is submitted through our website.

name: Latino Student Advisory Board
summary: The Latino Student Advisory Board aims to incorporate our ideas and/or suggestions into policy making at our University, state, and national level for the empowerment and advancement of the Latino community through public outreach, academic excellence,

name: Nevada Electric Racing
summary: The Nevada Electric Racing Club is an engineering competition team whose main goal is to design and manufacture a fully electric race car that will compete in the Formula SAE Electric competition.

name: A.B.L.E. Women
summary: We aim to empower women in multicultural backgrounds and to inspire service, scholarship, and leadership amongst our communities.

name: Business Student Council
summary: The Business Student Council advises the Dean and creates opportunities in alumni engagement, professional development, community service, student involvement, professional travel, socials and networking.

name: Management and Human Resources Association
summary: UNR MHRA is dedicated to expanding students’ exposure to career-oriented experiences and opportunities through student networking, leadership roles, and guest speakers.  The focus of this organization is Human Resources and Management disciplines.

name: American Society of Civil Engineers
summary: We provide hands-on opportunities to apply your engineering knowledge, networking opportunities with professionals, and prepare members for the transition from student to professionals.

name: Wolf Pack Racing
summary: Wolf Pack Racking is a club where the students design, manufacture, and race a single-seat off-road race car against other colleges worldwide.

name: Pre-PA Society Club
summary: The mission of the Pre-PA Society is to familiarize students with the Physician Assistant profession and the admission process through seminars, guest speakers, hands-on experience, critical thinking, and community service/engagement opportunities.

name: oSTEM at Nevada
summary: The purpose of oSTEM is to unite and support queer students and allies during their undergraduate and graduate studies of a STEM topic, and providing a continued network support to queer students into their STEM-focused careers.

name: Pi Tau Sigma Beta Nu
summary: Pi Tau Sigma is a Mechanical Engineering Honors Society which values and promotes integrity and leadership through academic, community, and professional service opportunities.

name: Nevada Microbiology Club
summary: Microbiology Club will engage its members in activities to promote their scientific and professional development as well as being a resource for members to connect with the microbiology community.

name: RenoACDA
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to advance vocal and choral music by providing resources and opportunities to develop students as professional musicians, leaders, educators and conductors.

name: Psi Chi
summary: Psi Chi is an honors society that recognizes and promotes the excellence in the science and application of psychology. Membership is open to students who are majoring or minoring in psychology. We are also paired with the Psychology Club.

name: Nevada Mock Trial Team
summary: The UNR Mock Trial Team is a competitive club that receives fictional criminal and civil cases and prepares those cases as both the attorneys and witnesses. Both semesters team members their case occasionally competing against other schools nationwide.

name: Turning Point USA
summary: Turning Point USA’s mission is to educate students about the importance of fiscal responsibility, free markets, and capitalism through innovative campus activism and non-partisan, thought-provoking discussion.

name: Nu Rho Psi
summary: Nu Rho Psi is the National Honor Society for neuroscience majors/minors. Nu Rho Psi is the only nationally recognized honor society in the area of brain research. We are the Nevada Chapter and primarily focus on excellence, scholarship and service.

name: UNR National Association for Music Education Collegiate
summary: UNR National Association for Music Education Collegiate is a club that offers Music Education majors the opportunity to learn more about our field, connect with the community, organize events and build relationships within the club.

name: NevadaTeach STEM Club
summary: A place where STEM students are able to come together and voice their ideas and create new friendships. Also a place for students pursuing STEM teaching degrees can create connections for their future classrooms.

name: American Institute of Chemical Engineers
summary: We are a group of students mainly focused around chemical engineering but welcoming to all majors. The club serves as the Nevada Chapter for the national AIChE organization. We seek to help get more people interested in the field of chemical engineering.

name: The UNR Film Club
summary: The UNR Film Club is dedicated to help inspire young artist create their voice through the art of filmmaking. In addition, we want to connect our members to those within the filmmaking community and find career development opportunities.

name: Phi Alpha Delta Law Fraternity, International
summary: P.A.D. is UNR's only pre-law professional organization and strives to provide students with well rounded preparation for a potential legal career with workshops, attorney and law school visits, and community service opportunities.

name: Society of Women Engineers
summary:
The UNR chapter of the society of women engineers provides a platform for professional
networking, youth outreach, and advocacy for gender equality in engineering.

name: Nu Alpha Kappa Fraternity, Inc
summary: We, NU ALPHA KAPPA, seek to unite and involve all students in a more harmonious and brotherly atmosphere through academic, social and cultural means.

name: Beta Alpha Psi - Zeta Iota
summary: Zeta Iota is a chapter of Beta Alpha Psi, an honorary accounting society. Membership in the Zeta Iota chapter gives our members access to essential tools to gain experience in their field of interest.

name: Association for Computing Machinery
summary: ACM's mission is to fuel passionate Computer Science students at UNR with opportunities to learn, network, and apply their knowledge in the real world.

name: Public Relations Student Society of America
summary: PRSSA connects you to a network of peers and professionals who will become lifelong friends and mentors. PRSSA facilitates the development of valuable relationships with future colleagues through PRSSA National/ regional events and PRSSA Chapters.

name: Alpha Kappa Psi
summary: Alpha Kappa Psi is a Professional Co-ed Business Fraternity founded in 1904 with the purpose of developing its members into principled business leaders.

summary: The UBAC is social and academic club created to promote and support academic interests and professional development of UNR students through research and professional opportunities as well as opportunity to engage with peers.

summary: The goal of this organization is to create a community (or familia) that emphasizes professional and academic support to students who are also passionate about increasing diversity/representation in their respective STEM fields.

name: Theta Tau
summary: Theta Tau is professional co-ed engineering fraternity whose purpose is to develop and maintain a high standard of professional interest among its members, and to unite them in a strong bond of fraternal fellowship.

name: Human Development and Family Sciences Club
summary: HDFS Club is a place where those majoring, minoring, or are simply interested in Human Development and Family Sciences can come together. We focus on giving back to the community along with connecting our members to outside organizations.

name: Manzanita - Juniper Halls
summary: Juniper Hall offers suites with single-resident rooms designed for returning and transfer students. Built in 1896, Manzanita Hall has undergone renovations to accommodate up to 100 residents.

name: Sierra Hall
summary: Sierra Hall includes singles and doubles designed primarily for returning and transfer students.

summary: Nevada Neuro Society (NNS) is a community health and outreach club focused on educating and helping people in the greater Reno area. NNS welcomes all members from all backgrounds while encouraging learning and exploration in neuroscience.

name: UNR Bridge Club
summary: If you love strategy and card games then bridge club is for you. Bridge is a card game that develops critical thinking, creativity, will help you get connections for your career and you can get new friends! We also play a lot of other card games!

summary: Wolf Pack Miracle Network at the University of Nevada, Reno strives to engage the campus community in leadership and service work while raising awareness and crucial funding for Renown Children’s Hospital.

name: John Mackay Club
summary: Promote within the Mackay School of Earth Sciences and Engineering a relationship between the earth science disciplines and foster a professional pride in these sciences

Mission Statement:
Educating others about the profession of speech language pathology and audiology and advocating for our profession by giving back to the community.

summary: The College of Business Career and Corporate Outreach center engages students with career events to support their advancement into the community.

name: Sigma Phi Epsilon
summary: Sigma Phi Epsilon, SigEp, is redefining fraternity on college campuses across the country. We complement a man’s education by delivering a premier student experience in one of the most formative times of his life.

summary: Our goals are to provide students at The University of Nevada, Reno with the necessary techniques to efficiently and effectively navigate financial markets. Club meetings include education on investments, discussion, and various financial competitions.

summary: We are the Young Democratic Socialists of Reno, centralized at the University of Nevada Reno. We focus on community service and socialist education.

name: NevadaCARES
summary: NevadaCARES aims to end power-based personal violence by engaging the campus community in awareness, education, programming, and transformative action.

summary: The mission of the Black Diaspora Program is to increase the outreach, recruitment, retention and, ultimately, graduation rates of Black students. Our principle focus is creating a positive atmosphere for student learning in conjunction with facilitating

summary: Multicultural events, programs, resources and services available for Indigenous Students at UNR.

summary: The Economics Club provides information to all those interested in studying Economics at the undergraduate level and beyond about opportunities available.Club events and forums help students explore possible career paths, grow econ knowledge and network.

summary: The Latino Research Center serves as a nexus between the Latino community and the University. Its mission is to foster research, student achievement, faculty collaboration, advocacy and outreach.

name: The Multicultural Center: Asian & Pacific Islander Programs
summary: Join to view upcoming events hosted by UNR's API Coordinator

summary: The Department of Physics at the University of Nevada, Reno specializes in atomic, molecular, optical and chemical physics, high energy density and plasma physics, condensed matter physics, and atmospheric sciences.

summary: The University Libraries, including the Mathewson-IGT Knowledge Center, DeLaMare Science and Engineering Library, and the Jon Bilbao Basque Library exists to advance teaching, research, student learning, and community engagement.

summary: The Gerontology Academic Program, Sanford Center for Aging, offers the interdisciplinary Gerontology Minor (18 credits) and Gerontology Certificate (24 credits),

summary: Our department is home to exciting research at the forefront of many areas of mechanical and aerospace engineering. In the last three years our faculty have received numerous impressive distinctions.

Dedicated to the values and commitments of the social work profession through education, research and public service

summary: The UNR Economics Department provides academic programs and events that enhance economic understanding.

summary: The University of Nevada, Reno Army Reserve Officers' Training Corps (ROTC) is one of the best leadership courses in the country and can be part of college curriculum.

summary: At the University of Nevada, Reno, Department of Music you can count on a culture of innovative music creation, engaging performances and high standards.

summary: Join a club dedicated to connecting students in wildlife and other environmental fields through hands-on experiences and professional networking. While we focus on wildlife, all are welcome to explore and connect with us!

summary: The HART Initiative utilizes the JAVA Memory Care program to alleviate loneliness and spread happiness by gathering college students to volunteer at local memory care facilities. HART aims to improve the quality of life for those with dementia.

summary: UNR Robotics hopes to create opportunities for its members to gain exposure in the robotics field through the club activities.

summary: The office supports students who are applying to nationally and internationally competitive scholarships and fellowships, such as Truman, Rhodes, Goldwater, Boren, Udall, Marshall, NSF GRFP, NSF REU, etc.

summary: The American Nuclear Society (ANS) is an organization for those who embrace the nuclear sciences and technologies for their vital contributions to improving people’s lives and preserving the planet. We are the UNR student chapter.

summary: Speech Pathology and Audiology offers a pre-professional B.S. degree in Speech Pathology and Audiology and professional graduate degrees in Speech-Language Pathology.

summary: The Honor Society of Phi Kappa Phi is an honor society established in 1897 to recognize and encourage superior scholarship without restriction as to area of study, and to promote the "unity and democracy of education."

name: Organizational Resilience

summary: The Nevada Debate Team is an intercollegiate competitive speech and debate program in the college of Liberal Arts at the University of Nevada, Reno.

summary: Office of Marketing and Communications

summary: Part of the College of Business at the University of Nevada, Reno

summary: The purpose of Neuroscience Outreach Club is to promote outreach, research, education, training and development of graduate students in the realm of Neuroscience at the University of Nevada, Reno.

summary: Academic department at UNR with undergraduate programs in Political Science and International Affairs and and graduate programs in Political Science and Public Administration.

summary: Our mission is to promote the diversity of world cultures by offering an educational experience that gives graduate and undergraduate students not just a window to the world, but a passkey to understanding and communicating with its peoples in meaningful

summary: NAPS is made up of graduate students from the clinical psychology doctoral program in addition to graduate students from other programs. The goal of NAPS is to represent the student body and provide graduate programs with input from the students.

summary: The EECB Outreach Club is dedicated to fostering interactions between the graduate program in Ecology, Evolution, and Conservation Biology and the University of Nevada, Reno and surrounding communities with the goal of expanding scientific knowledge.

summary: We, the graduate students of the University of Nevada, Reno Mechanical Engineering Department, recognizing the importance of collaboration and community among young professionals in our field, do hereby establish a graduate student club.

summary: The PSGSO represents all graduate students and promotes the welfare of graduate students within the Department of Political Science. PSGSO also organizes various cultural and academic activities sponsored by GSA.

summary: The UNR Institute for Neuroscience is the organizational home of neuroscience research, training, and outreach for neuroscience.

summary: The Office of Digital Learning provides faculty support, training, resources and consultations for academic technology, course design and delivery, and online learning.

summary: The purpose and aim of the club are to provide opportunities for graduate level social work students to engage in both the UNR and surrounding communities. The club will organize social, community service, and professional development events.

summary: The Social Psychology Club is comprised of members of the interdisciplinary social psychology graduate program, as well as others interested in the area of social psychology. The club offers educational, social, and service events to its members.

summary: ACDI is an organization established to promote public awareness of issues concerning democracy and human rights in Iran while advancing the liaison of the Iranian diaspora to strengthen an independent academic alliance for a democratic and free Iran.

summary: This is for the Communication Studies department. This will help organize events for faculty, and staff in Communication Studies.

summary: The Judicial Studies Graduate Degree Program at the University of Nevada, Reno offers several advanced degrees designed exclusively for (a) sitting judges who (b) have completed a J.D. degree from an ABA accredited law school.

summary: WIM-UNR, is a club created for educational purposes that guides its members toward the knowledge of mineral resources and related industries, advocates for a more diverse, equitable, inclusive industry and promote professional development and networking.

summary: Our mission is to promote Glioblastoma Multiforme research by raising awareness and funds, all while fostering a positive and hopeful community through our events and social media presence.

summary: The University of Nevada's Global Water Center is a response to societal demands for creative, integrative approaches in solving complex issues related to water resources. The Center's mission is to translate science into actionable solutions accessible

summary: The UWSC supports all students with reading, writing, presenting, and creating to learn and communicate. We also contribute directly to the English Corequisite program and support international students with language placement and language learning.

summary:
To create an even stronger Pre-Health community on our campus​

To create a home for our Pre-Health students​

To promote collaboration and teamwork amongst Pre-Health students.

summary: The Nevada First-Gen Network (NFGN) is dedicated to providing relief for first-generation students affected by COVID-19, through community outreach, network building, micro-grant distribution, and professional development.

summary: NSA is a nonprofit organization and unites all the Nepalese students, postdocts,f aculties and families to foster culture and tradition

name: Western Association of Student Financial Aid Administrators
summary: WASFAA is a community of more than 1,000 financial aid administrators and professionals dedicated to helping students reach their postsecondary education goals. We represent professionals in 8 states in the Western Region.

name: Touhou Project Club
summary: The Touhou Project club is dedicated to exploring and enjoying the games, manga, music, fanworks, and other media of the indie series Touhou.

name: WOLFPACK BATTALION
summary: UNR's ROTC shapes ethical leaders through rigorous training since 1888. Focused on leadership, academics, character, fitness, community, military readiness, and adaptability, it has 120+ active Cadets, contributing to its enduring Legacy.

name: WolfCard Office
summary: The WolfCard can be used to access many on-campus services including meal swipes, campus purchases, access to the Recreation Center, checking out materials in the Mathewson-IGT Knowledge Center, libraries, and other services.

name: Diversity, Social Justice, and Inclusion
summary: DSJI is an organization housed in the RLHFS department working to provide diverse and inclusive educational activities and experiences to all students, but particularly geared towards students living on campus.

name: Mackay Rockhounds
summary: Mackay Rockhounds is a club dedicated to earth science and exploration. Charted by AIPG, our club aims to foster a community passionate about geology, geography, and nature.

name: University of Nevada, Reno Roller Coaster Club
summary: Bringing like minded individuals together who have a passion for amusement parks, roller coasters, the design process and competition.

name: Payment 4 Placements - UNR
summary: P4P is a nationwide movement calling for all social work students to be paid for their degree-required internships.

name: Healthcare Administration and Leadership Student Association
summary: The Healthcare Administration and Leaders Student Association (HALSA) at the University of Nevada, Reno is committed to fostering a dynamic community of aspiring healthcare professionals dedicated to excellence in leadership, administration, and service.

name: AsPIre
summary: AsPIre was founded in 2021 at the University of Nevada, Reno as an alliance of Asian, Asian American and Indigenous Pacific Islander (AAPI) faculty and staff members who are interested in shaping and advancing AAPI initiatives on campus and addressing re

name: Nevada Timber Strong
summary: The student competition is based on creating a sustainable, 2-story wood light-framed building. This club is tasked with designing and building an artistically creative building that is sustainable, aesthetically-pleasing, and structurally durable.

name: Advancements in Teaching Excellence
summary: We collaborate with instructors across the University in professional development as teachers through events and programs that connect instructors with resources, build community, and encourage reflective practice towards teaching excellence.

name: The Multicultural Center: LGBTQIA2S+
summary: The mission of Pride Student Services is to empower and support the lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer, intersex and asexual community and their allies by promoting acceptance and understanding at the University of Nevada, Reno and in Northern NV

name: Center for the Application of Substance Abuse Technologies
summary: CASAT’s primary mission is to improve prevention and treatment services for individuals with addictive behaviors by helping states, organizations, students, and the existing workforce apply research-based practices.

name: GSA Judicial, Budget & Finance Committee
summary: The Mission of the GSA Judicial, Budget, and Finance Committee (JB&F) is to review and amend the Constitution and Bylaws when needed. GSA policies are also reviewed by JB&F.

name: The School of Social Work Student Advisory Board
summary: SWSAB serves to honor the voices of all students and to bridge the connection with faculty to further develop the School of Social Work.

name: GSA Student Advocacy Committee
summary:
The mission of the Student Advocacy Committee is to advocate for the rights and
needs of graduate students and act as a liaison between the students and upper
administration to resolve student issues.

See our section on graduate resources!

name: GSA Events Committee
summary:
The mission of the GSA Events Committee is to coordinate events where Graduate
Students are able to come together and socialize, including but not limited to the
Welcome Back Social, the Fall and Spring Socials, and Graduate Appreciation
Week.

name: Nevada Scholars of Tomorrow
summary: A student-led organization focused on mentorship and community build for African American/Black incoming freshman and new students. Created to aid students on their path to success at the University of Nevada, Reno.

name: UNR Mathematics Club
summary: A hub for enthusiasts of math, both majors and non-majors, to meet friends, network, prepare for grad school, and other activities on math or math adjacent paths

name: Pack Life Admin Group
summary: This organization meets monthly to discuss improvement to the UNR Pack Life software.

name: Educational Leadership
summary: Graduate-level program in the UNR College of Education and Human Development

name: The Biggest Little Shadow Cast
summary: The Biggest Little Shadow Cast will shadow the onscreen performances of the 1975 movie version of The Rocky Horror Picture Show.

name: Ice Skating Allies
summary: Ice Allies fosters an inclusive community, empowering adaptive athletes to gain confidence, overcome challenges, and inspire others through the joy of ice skating, creating opportunities for personal growth and connection.

name: Golden Scholars Program
summary: Providing first-generation students in the College of Science with support and resources to navigate college life.

name: Care Club
summary: Care Club's goals is to create events and fundraise for causes involving individuals with cognitive disabilities. Members are expected to volunteer and support the community for nurturing the ideas of inclusiveness.

name: GSA Summer Task Force
summary: The GSA Summer Task Force is a team of graduate student leaders who will serve throughout the summer to support organizational priorities, including club and organization recognition, student representative advertising, and PackLife promotion.

name: Polymath Wannabes - UNR Chapter
summary: fcdu/ug

name: UNR Linguistics Club
summary: The UNR Linguistics Club is an organization interested in the study of language in all its aspects. It aims to consider and discuss all ideas relevant to linguistics and to disseminate these ideas to others.

name: The Meteorology and Climatology Club
summary: MaCC aims to assist in the professional development and continuing education of club members, to allow for opportunities for interaction and networking for students, and to take part in outreach activities.

name: Pakistani Student Association (Undergraduate Chapter)
summary: The Pakistani Student Association (PSA) celebrates and promotes Pakistani culture through events, community service, and campus collaborations. PSA fosters unity, cultural awareness, and a supportive network for students of all backgrounds.

name: Wolf Pack AI Collective
summary: Human-centered AI education club aimed at inclusive development of AI literacy for UNR students of all backgrounds. We aim to demystify AI, invoke critical thinking and innovation, and embrace the uncertainty of a rapidly advancing technological world.

name: Pack Pilots
summary: Pack Pilots brings together students who dream of flight—whether as pilots, engineers, or enthusiasts—to share experiences, explore aviation, and build lifelong connections grounded in Wolf Pack pride.

name: Distributive Education Clubs of America
summary: DECA is an international organization for college students that prepares emerging leaders and entrepreneurs for careers in marketing, finance, hospitality, and management. It provides hands-on, real-world experiences, competitive events, and leadership.

name: Students Against ICE Abuses
summary: Students aiming to raise awareness of Immigration Customs and Enforcement misconduct and mismanagement, specifically on K-12 school grounds.

name: Nevada Origami
summary: We are an inclusive student organization dedicated to sharing the joys of origami with beginners and seasoned folders alike.

name: University of Nevada Thousand Pound Club
summary: To foster student-athletes committed to excellence in Weightlifting and Powerlifting by providing structured training, knowledgeable instruction, and a culture of progress, accountability, perseverance, and mental fortitude.

name: Women In Aviation
summary: The mission of the Women in Aviation Club to promote the advancement of women in all aviation and aerospace career fields and interests, provide networking and mentorship opportunities, support professional development, and foster a welcoming community

name: Reproductive Freedom for All UNR
summary: RFFA at UNR is a student led club that is focused in education and advocacy, centered on community engagement aiming for reproductive freedom for all. The club is an inclusive space for learning and discussions, as well as civic action.

name: UNR AIAA
summary: UNR Student Chapter for the American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics

name: UNR Wargaming Club
summary: The UNR Wargaming Club is dedicated to building a community around tabletop strategy gaming, with a focus on Games Workshop titles such as Warhammer 40k and Age of Sigmar. Members engage in both painting and gameplay, fostering creativity, strategy.

name: Wolf Pack Politics and Policy Club
summary: The Wolf Pack Politics and Policy Club is a non-partisan student organization for students interested in politics and policy. The organization seeks to expand students’ knowledge and engagement at the local, state, national, and international levels.

name: Tiny Tots Health Initiative
summary: Tiny Tots Health Initiative is a health-based volunteering club focused on helping our pediatric community. Through creative, engaging programs and partnerships with clinics, and our community, we make health education accessible, fun, and impactful.
